description: FANOS canonical wire KATs (spec §7.1–§7.5). Byte strings are lower-case hex.

varint.comment: QUIC variable-length integers, minimal-length. The top two bits of the first byte select the length (1/2/4/8 bytes); the value is big-endian in the remaining bits.

field_element_width.comment: Bytes per GF(q) element = ceil(log2 q / 8). Elements are big-endian, high bits zero.

point_encoding.comment: A projective point/line is three canonical field elements concatenated. GF(7) uses one byte each.

byte_string.comment: varint length prefix followed by the bytes.

frame.comment: frame = type:varint || length:varint || body[length]. Types are grouped by high nibble.

capability_negotiation.comment: spec §7.4: version is a single monotonically-increasing profile number; two peers operate at min(version) and the bitwise-AND intersection of capabilities. CORE is the one mandatory bit (baseline DHT + Direct-profile routing, §7.6) every conformant node sets; an intersection that comes out empty is the handshake's capability-incompatibility condition (distinct from ordinary feature degradation, e.g. a peer simply lacking APHANTOS_FULL).
capability_negotiation.capability_flags_comment: bit position (LSB-first) of each named flag in the u32 capabilities bitfield.

capability_intersection_examples.comment: spec §7.4's own example: a DHT-only (CORE-only) node interoperates with a full node.
disjoint optional-only sets — the genuine incompatibility condition.

hello_handshake.comment: spec §7.3/§7.4 self-certifying HELLO body layout: version(2 BE) || capabilities(4 BE) || field_q(4 BE) || epoch(8 BE) || coord(12) || proof(80), wrapped as a HELLO(0x00) frame. `proof` here is an OPAQUE PLACEHOLDER (repeated 0xAB bytes), NOT a verified VRF proof — this vector pins the field layout, order, and framing, which is what the codec must reproduce bit-for-bit; cryptographic validity of a real proof is exercised separately by fanos-quic's own tests (crates/fanos-quic/src/identity.rs), which cross-check that a REAL hello_bytes() output's non-proof fields decode to this same layout.

hello.proof_placeholder_hex: ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab ab

hello_ack.comment: HELLO_ACK(0x01) body = version(2 BE) || capabilities(4 BE) — echoes the negotiated (min version, intersected capabilities), sent once the receiver has verified the peer's HELLO proof and computed the intersection.

error_no_reason.comment: ERROR(0x05) body = code:varint || reason:bytes (spec §7.5). Sent on version/capability incompatibility, with no reason text.

error_with_reason.comment: ERROR carrying explanatory UTF-8 text after the code — the reason's length is bounded by the enclosing frame's own length field, so no separate inner length prefix is needed.

tessera_layout.comment: Fixed-size sealed onion (§5.7, §7.7); constant total for length-indistinguishability. The holonomy path-authenticator is carried ENCRYPTED inside the innermost DELIVER command, NEVER a cleartext header field — a constant tag at a fixed offset would be a perfect cross-hop correlator (audit A1). Mirrors fanos_aphantos::sealed.

cleartext_header.name: len_ct
cleartext_header.note: AEAD(u16 body_len) = 2 + 16 tag; the real layer length, encrypted

tessera_layout.encrypted_body: body_ct = AEAD(cmd || inner) then keystream padding to total_bytes; cmd = (DELIVER || holonomy(32)) | (NEXT || next_coord(12))

reject.comment: A conformant decoder MUST reject every one of these as non-canonical.

cases.why: value 7 is out of range in GF(7)
the zero vector is not a projective point

error_taxonomy.comment: Protocol error codes (§7.5), grouped by class digit.
